commodities.name: Wheat
commodities.detail: Black Sea and Persian Gulf shipping routes disrupted. Hormuz closure blocking fertilizer shipments at peak planting season.

commodities.name: Corn
commodities.detail: Fertilizer supply chain from Middle East severely disrupted. Diesel costs for farming operations surging.

commodities.name: Soybeans
commodities.detail: Shipping insurance costs quadrupled through Strait of Hormuz. Asian import routes rerouting via Cape of Good Hope.

commodities.name: Rice
commodities.detail: Asian import routes through Persian Gulf completely disrupted. Export restrictions emerging in Southeast Asia.

commodities.name: Sugar
commodities.detail: Energy costs for refineries surging globally. Transport costs up 40% due to diesel price spikes.

commodities.name: Cooking Oil
commodities.detail: Palm oil shipping through Strait of Hormuz blocked. Alternative routing adds 12+ days and $933,000 per voyage.

commodities.name: Coffee
commodities.detail: Global shipping costs and fuel surcharges rising sharply. Container rates up 85% on key routes.

commodities.name: Beef
commodities.detail: Feed costs up due to grain price spikes. Diesel-dependent supply chain passing costs to consumers.

context: Food prices accelerating as Strait of Hormuz remains effectively closed to tanker traffic (Day 8), disrupting 33% of globally traded fertilizer at start of Northern Hemisphere planting season. Shipping rerouted via Cape of Good Hope adds $933,000/voyage and doubles transit times. Diesel at $4.65/gal driving transport costs sharply higher. Grocery costs up ~10% week-over-week. USDA warns of potential supply shortages within 2-3 weeks if shipping disruption persists.
